slides.title: Surgical Precision 🔪
slides.content:
Sometimes you don't want to make *everything* optional. Sometimes you just want to grab a few specific properties from a giant Interface and completely ignore the rest.

This is incredibly common when passing props down to UI components.

slides.title: The `Pick<Type, Keys>` Utility
slides.content: `Pick` lets you construct a new type by picking a specific set of properties from an existing one.

quizData.question: If you try to add `id: 1` to the `user` object above perfectly typed as `PublicUser`, what logically happens?

options.text: It silently drops the ID at runtime.
TypeScript throws a compile error because 'id' does not exist on the elegantly precise new Picked type.

quizData.explanation: Because you specifically Picked `"name" | "email"`, the new structure explicitly excludes `id`.

slides.title: The `Omit<Type, Keys>` Utility
slides.content: `Omit` is the exact inverse of Pick. Rather than stating what you want, you state exactly what you fundamentally want to delete or exclude.

slides.title: When to Use Which? 🤔
slides.content: Use `Pick` when you only need a small handful of properties. Use `Omit` when you want almost everything but deeply need to hide one or two sensitive keys.
